title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: Audit run 2026-04-26. Timeboxed. Records every package/resource checked, verdict rubric, link, one-line note.

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: package “covers” spatial distribution dynamics space four hold: - () named exported function worked vignette computes spatial Markov conditional transition matrices; - (b) feature work release last 24 months (just reactive ecosystem-fix commits); - (c) CRAN, stated production intent discoverable docs (vignette, pkgdown, equivalent); - (d) used anyone outside author’s lab — citations downstream packages depending count. Failing (c) (d) means package methodological infrastructure authors, user-facing product. can serve oracle close gap griddy-audience perspective. gap decomposed three independent classes: 1. Methodological gap — R code compute matrices ? 2. Workflow gap — sf/tidy-native, ID/time semantics, map output? 3. Product gap — CRAN-grade R package non-author discover, install, use?

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: Pass. maintained, CRAN-grade, tidy/sf-native R package covers spatial distribution dynamics. Two academic-supplement packages (estdaR, spdyn) implement methods serve useful oracles, neither positioned user product: estdaR’s README explicitly states plans go CRAN; accompanies two 2020 papers. spdyn R-Forge twelve years version 0.0-3, single maintainer, vignette, CRAN. packages cited heavily prior art. Neither competes audience griddy target. applied-prototype gate still right next step — methodological landscape unclear, audience risk remains first-order risk. prototype’s job prove workflow useful non-methodologists.

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: Repo: https://github.com/amvallone/estdaR Authors: Vallone (UCN Chile), Le Gallo, Chasco, Ayouba — credible spatial econometrics researchers; Le Gallo Rey collaborator Last commit: 2024-03-24 (~25 months stale) Distribution: GitHub , CRAN, R-universe Imports: sf, spdep, ggplot2, gridExtra, RColorBrewer, gasfluxes (last odd) Exported functions (17): mkv, sp.mkv, lisamkv, sig.lisamkv, d.LISA, quad, discret, sp.tau, tau, theta, prais, shorrock, st.st, homo.test, sp.homo.test, moran, geary, join.d. Map griddy v0.1 + v0.2 + deferred: Coverage: methodologically broader proposed griddy v0.1. Workflow check (read source mkv.R, sp.mkv.R, lisamkv.R): - Input: numeric matrix, n units × t periods. long-form, ID/time column semantics. - Weights: spdep::listw . tidy weights API. - Output: base R lists matrices. tibble, sf preservation, plot methods. - Documentation: rough — “later..”, typos, minimal examples, vignettes, pkgdown site. - Visualization: none, despite ggplot2 Imports. Additional API-level friction runtime pass: - us48 ships sf object, documented workflow immediately strips geometry wide matrix. sf hard dependency without sf-preserving analysis path. - Row identity implicit. Results keyed matrix row position, id/time, sorting filtering analysis makes “unit moved?” hard recover. - lisamkv$move stores 16 origin-destination LISA transitions integer codes without human-readable labels HH -> HL. - sp.mkv() labels conditioning states Lag 1 Lag 5, surface spatial-lag cutpoints behind labels. - Results bare lists, S3 classes print(), summary(), plot(), tidy coercion methods. - Normalization steps per-year relative income left user incantations rather exposed helpers. - library(estdaR) masks common spdep functions including geary moran. Verdict rubric: () ✓, (b) ✗ (last commit reactive rgdal dependency removal forced spdep, feature work; feature work since 2020 paper publication), (c) ✗ (README explicitly disclaims CRAN intent; positioned paper supplement), (d) unclear — citations 2020 papers don’t translate package usage. Methodological oracle, product. Useful cross-validation. competition.

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: Project: https://r-forge.r-project.org/projects/spdyn/ Site: https://spdyn.r-forge.r-project.org/ Maintainer: Osmar Loaiza Last commit: 2024-10-28, message “Updated functions compatibility ‘sf’ objects” Distribution: R-Forge, version 0.0-3, installable via install.packages("spdyn", repos="http://R-Forge.R-project.org"). CRAN. Exported functions (R/ directory listing): markov, spMarkov, mfpt, steadyState, clusterQuadrant, unimoran, unimoran.test, bimoran, bimoran.test, lisa.perm, bilisa.perm, roseDiagram, moran.scatterplot, initState, spInitStates, plus print/plot methods (plot.lisaPerm, print.moranPerm). Map griddy proposal: plot methods (plot.lisaPerm, moran.scatterplot) — narrower visualization griddy proposes, estdaR . Verdict rubric: () ✓, (b) ✗ (Oct 2024 commit sf-compat fix — reactive-maintenance pattern estdaR; feature work years), (c) ✗ (R-Forge , twelve years version 0.0-3, CRAN, vignette, pkgdown), (d) low — single-maintainer academic infrastructure negligible discoverability. Methodological oracle, product. Cite prior art validate . proposed alternative name spdyn still occupied — drop candidate list regardless.

what: rgeoda — covers LISA but not LISA Markov

text: Anselin’s R bindings GeoDa libgeoda. CRAN, actively maintained. Inspected R/lisa.R directly: 21 functions, cross-sectional LISA variants (local Moran, Geary, Getis-Ord, Join Count, quantile LISA, neighbor match test). spatial Markov, LISA Markov, temporal regime transitions. kill-check prior pass negative. Verdict: () ✗ fail. competitor.

text: CRAN, “Continuous-Lag Spatial Markov Chains”. categorical random fields (geostatistics-style — soil types, lithology). regional distribution dynamics. Verdict: scope. Different method family despite name overlap.

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: CRAN, “Spatial Absorbing Markov Chains”. Landscape ecology / connectivity modeling absorbing states mortality. regional distribution dynamics. Verdict: scope. Note name-collision risk: CRAN package “spatial” + “Markov” name occupies adjacent search territory.

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: CRAN, active. Multistate Markov biostatistics panel data. spatial conditioning. Different audience entirely. Verdict: scope. Listed completeness.

what: What this changes about the brief
title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: Methodological-gap framing mostly fine needs precision. brief’s claim “R appear cohesive, modern equivalent PySAL giddy” defensible “modern equivalent” means CRAN-grade tidy/sf-native package. misleading read “R code exists.” Tighten : “R academic-supplement implementations methods (estdaR, spdyn) maintained, CRAN-grade, tidy/sf-native package. griddy targets package gap, methods gap.” spdyn taken name. Cross alternative-name list regardless. Validation strategy gains free oracle. PySAL giddy remains primary oracle. Add: cross-check estdaR::mkv(), estdaR::sp.mkv(), estdaR::lisamkv(), spdyn::spMarkov() outputs inputs. Three independent implementations agreeing strong evidence new code correct. Disagreements estdaR/spdyn match giddy documentable; disagreements three bugs. Etiquette: cite prior R-side work, competing products. packages appear README’s “Related work” / “Prior art” section one-line summaries links. Neither maintainer needs consulted building parallel package targeting different audience — ’s standard pattern paper supplements coexist user-facing packages. Don’t try revive estdaR. Earlier audit suggested opening maintainer issue amvallone/estdaR. Withdraw . README explicitly disclaims CRAN intent; tidy/sf refactor PR nowhere land reaches R users. logic spdyn — twelve years 0.0-3 R-Forge clear signal maintainer pursuing user-product trajectory. Differentiation framing. “End--end sf/tidy workflows”, “applied-question framing”, “CRAN distribution”, “modern visualization defaults” — differentiators methodological status quo, “use Python, use academic GitHub package, write .” reasonable user-product gap address. load-bearing differentiators classification helper, ID/time-aware inputs, explicit weight/lag metadata, labeled transition outputs, S3 result objects. Visualization helps, core value exists even users never plot. Oracle fixtures. estdaR numerics look usable oracle fixtures. Snapshot stable outputs us48/state-income workflows static CSV/RDS files, validate PySAL giddy, keep ordinary CI independent estdaR Python.

title: Gap Audit: griddy
text: audit: applied prototype clearly demonstrates audience value (.e., non-methodologist reading “neighborhood actually move?” finds compelling) → build griddy CRAN-targeted standalone package. README must cite estdaR spdyn prior R-side work. prototype slog audience reaction flat → package. methods already exist academic R; without audience pulling , third academic-style package adds nothing. intermediate “contribute upstream instead” path closed — neither prior package positioned absorb work.

title: Supporting Gap Audit Notes: R Support For Geospatial Distribution Dynamics
text: Original outcome: partial pass. Revised comparing audit.md: workflow/product pass R-side prior art. gap narrower initial scope brief implied. R GitHub package, amvallone/estdaR, directly implements substantial part PySAL spatial dynamics family: classic Markov matrices, spatial Markov matrices, LISA Markov matrices, significant-state LISA Markov, spatial homogeneity tests, steady-state distributions, mobility measures. said, find maintained CRAN package provides cohesive, modern sf/tidy workflow geospatial distribution dynamics comparable PySAL giddy. standalone package case therefore reframed “R lacks methods” “R lacks maintained, documented, sf-native, visualization-forward workflow methods.”

title: Supporting Gap Audit Notes: R Support For Geospatial Distribution Dynamics
text: sfdep closest maintained spatial-analysis neighbor. stated purpose provide sf tidyverse-friendly interface spdep, core functionality around neighbors, weights, spatial lags, local indicators spatial association. reference index includes spacetime support, emerging hotspot analysis, local_moran(), st_lag(), neighbors, weights, spatial Markov, LISA Markov, transition matrices, regional distribution dynamics. Sources: https://sfdep.josiahparry.com/ https://search.r-project.org/CRAN/refmans/sfdep/html/00Index.html https://rdrr.io/cran/sfdep/man/local_moran.html Implication: sfdep supplies several required building blocks may right upstream home one two verbs, appear cover full workflow.

title: Supporting Gap Audit Notes: R Support For Geospatial Distribution Dynamics
text: estdaR important audit result. GitHub package exploratory spatio-temporal data analysis R appears directly aimed methodological family. function index includes: mkv(): Markov transition probability matrix sp.mkv(): spatial Markov transition probability matrix, citing Rey (2001) lisamkv(): LISA Markov transition matrix sig.lisamkv(): LISA Markov non-significant state sp.homo.test(): homogeneity tests spatial Markov transition probabilities sp.tau(): global indicators mobility association st.st(), prais(), shorrock(), theta(), related rank/mobility helpers package README says still development intended CRAN short run. repository releases, 11 stars, docs indicate version 0.0.0.9000. Documentation pages sparse places, several “later” details sections. API matrix/listw oriented rather sf/tidy long-data oriented. Sources: https://github.com/amvallone/estdaR https://rdrr.io/github/amvallone/estdaR/ https://rdrr.io/github/amvallone/estdaR/man/sp.mkv.html https://rdrr.io/github/amvallone/estdaR/src/R/sp.mkv.R https://rdrr.io/github/amvallone/estdaR/man/lisamkv.html https://rdrr.io/github/amvallone/estdaR/man/sig.lisamkv.html Implication: future package must cite compare estdaR; claim first R implementation spatial Markov LISA Markov.

Sources: https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/markovchain/versions/0.10.3 Implication: use comparator optional validation source, replacement.

Sources: https://pysal.org/giddy/ https://pysal.org/giddy/notebooks/MarkovBasedMethods.html https://pysal.org/giddy/generated/giddy.markov.Spatial_Markov.html Implication: still right oracle, estdaR become second R-side comparator.

title: griddy
text: griddy experimental R package geospatial distribution dynamics sf tidy data. inspired PySAL giddy established spatial distribution dynamics work, especially Rey (2001). methods-novelty project. Prior R implementations exist, including estdaR spdyn. package target maintained, CRAN-grade, long-data, map-ready workflow. Current scope: classify longitudinal spatial values comparable classes estimate classic Markov transition matrices estimate spatial Markov transition matrices conditioned spatial lag class compute simple rank mobility return tidy tables ggplot2 plots API scope still provisional.

title: griddy
griddy
text: griddy keeps workflow keyed explicit id, time, value columns instead matrix row position. analytical outputs preserve transition labels, class intervals, spatial-lag intervals can inspected, joined, plotted without reverse-engineering array dimensions.

title: griddy
code: library(griddy) library(dplyr) library(spData) library(spdep) library(tidyr)  data(us_states, package = "spData") data(us_states_df, package = "spData")  states <- us_states |>   left_join(us_states_df, by = c("NAME" = "state")) |>   filter(!NAME %in% c("Alaska", "Hawaii", "Puerto Rico")) |>   arrange(NAME)  panel <- states |>   select(NAME, median_income_10, median_income_15, geometry) |>   pivot_longer(starts_with("median_income_"), names_to = "year", values_to = "income") |>   mutate(year = if_else(year == "median_income_10", 2010L, 2015L))  nb <- poly2nb(states, queen = TRUE)  classes <- classify_dynamics(panel, NAME, year, income, k = 5) classic <- markov_dynamics(classes, NAME, year, class) spatial <- spatial_markov(panel, NAME, year, income, nb = nb, k = 5)  classic$transitions |> select(id, from_time, to_time, transition) |> head() lag_intervals(spatial)

title: Classify Longitudinal Values For Distribution Dynamics — classify_dynamics
text: data data frame sf object long format. id, time, value Columns identifying spatial unit, time, value. method Classification method. "pooled_quantile" uses one set quantile breaks across periods. "time_quantile" uses period-specific ranks. "fixed" uses user-supplied breaks. "existing" treats value already classified state. k Number quantile classes. breaks Breaks method = "fixed". labels Optional class labels.

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: package design, verify gap directly. Required checks: Inspect current sfdep source docs temporal regime-transition workflows, spatial Markov chains, LISA Markov equivalents. Search CRAN, GitHub, R-universe, package docs “spatial Markov”, “LISA Markov”, “regime transition”, “regional income dynamics”, “Rey 2001”, “distribution dynamics”. Check Sergio Rey’s repositories paper supplements R reference implementations. Check whether examples exist spdep, sfdep, teaching packages, dissertation repos, chapter packages substantially reduce need package. Check whether markovchain plus existing spdep/sfdep examples already provide enough glue package thin. Possible audit outcomes: Pass: maintained R package covers spatial Markov geospatial distribution dynamics cohesive sf workflow. Proceed applied .Rmd prototype revisit package scope. Partial pass: R functions workflow, visualization, ACS/public-data ergonomics. Consider workflow package, pkgdown site, sfdep contribution rather full standalone package. Fail: maintained package clear package family already covers space. build standalone package; write notes, contribute upstream, stop. rest document assumes audit passes partially passes.

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: conditional v0.1 scoping document proposed R package, tentatively named griddy. package bring focused subset PySAL giddy-style geospatial distribution dynamics workflows R’s sf/tidy data ecosystem. intent methodological novelty. intent ecosystem translation, ergonomic integration, visualization-first workflows longitudinal spatial data. Reviewer questions: R gap real, relevant methods already well covered existing R packages? v0.1 scope correctly narrow? better standalone package, extension existing R package, tutorial/workflow site? start package, worked sfdep + markovchain article proves audience value first? function surface coherent R users? PySAL giddy oracle strategy strong enough? deferred avoid becoming unfocused PySAL clone? etiquette naming issues building R package inspired giddy? Push back hard. prior LLM-assisted gap analyses overstate gaps, especially mature geospatial econometrics ecosystems.

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: PySAL giddy Python package geospatial distribution dynamics: analysis spatial units move distributions time, movements relate spatial context. canonical examples regional income dynamics: states, counties, regions classified income quantiles time, analysts ask whether places transition upward, downward, remain trapped, converge, diverge, change spatial association regimes. methods descriptive exploratory rather predictive. sit spatial exploratory data analysis, economic mobility analysis, temporal visualization. R strong ingredients: sf, terra, stars spatial data spdep sfdep spatial weights local spatial statistics markovchain generic Markov chain methods TraMineR sequence analysis ggplot2, tmap, gganimate, plotly visualization tidycensus longitudinal ACS-style public data workflows spopt PySAL-inspired spatial optimization R Working hypothesis audit: R code computes many methods, appear cohesive, maintained, CRAN-grade equivalent PySAL giddy: package treats spatial distribution dynamics first-class workflow sf objects, tidy long data, transition matrices, spatial Markov models, regime transitions, rank mobility, map-ready output. now workflow/product hypothesis, methodological-availability hypothesis. next gate applied prototype: workflow feel useful non-methodologists, package add little beyond existing academic implementations.

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: Primary references: Rey, S. J. (2001). Spatial empirics economic growth convergence. Geographical Analysis, 33(3), 195-214. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1538-4632.2001.tb00444.x Rey, S. J., Kang, W., & Wolf, L. (2016). properties tests spatial effects discrete Markov chain models regional income distribution dynamics. Journal Geographical Systems, 18(4), 377-398. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10109-016-0234-x Kang, W., Rey, S., Stephens, P., Malizia, N., Wolf, L. J., Lumnitz, S., Gaboardi, J., Laura, J., Schmidt, C., Knaap, E., & Eschbacher, . (2019). pysal/giddy: giddy 2.2.1. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3351744 PySAL giddy documentation notebooks: https://pysal.org/notebooks/explore/giddy/intro.html https://pysal.org/giddy/api.html Core method families PySAL giddy: Classic discrete Markov chains class transitions time Spatial Markov chains, transition probabilities conditioned spatial lag classes LISA Markov chains, states local Moran / Moran scatterplot regimes Full-rank geographic-rank Markov methods Mobility measures derived transition matrices Rank mobility local indicators mobility association Directional LISA rose diagrams Alignment-based sequence methods

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: Working name: griddy. Rationale: Memorable adjacent giddy without reusing name Suggests grids, spatial units, movement Light enough visualization-forward package Risks: name derivative giddy; acceptable attribution explicit package positioned R implementation inspired PySAL giddy. “Griddy” also refers dance/celebration, technical issue may affect searchability. Alternative names: geodynr spmobility sptransition lisadyn Current preference: keep griddy working name . Re-evaluate public commits. use spdyn; name already occupied R-Forge package implementing exploratory space-time dynamics methods.

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: Proposed v0.1 user-facing functions: Approximate count: 8 user-facing functions. still upper bound. implementation pressure high, cut steady_state() trajectory-related output cutting spatial_markov(). Naming unresolved. example intentionally drops grd_ prefix heavy prefixing may feel unidiomatic R grd suggests raster grids. collision risk becomes real, reconsider prefix package name final.
code: # Classification classify_dynamics(data, id, time, value,                   method = c("pooled_quantile", "year_quantile", "fixed"),                   k = 5, breaks = NULL, labels = NULL)  # Classic Markov markov_dynamics(classes, id, time, state) transition_matrix(x, type = c("probability", "count")) steady_state(x)  # Spatial Markov spatial_markov(data, id, time, value, nb,                k = 5, lag_k = 5,                class_method = "pooled_quantile")  # Rank mobility rank_mobility(data, id, time, value,               compare = c("adjacent", "endpoint"))  # Visualization plot_transition_matrix(x) plot_spatial_markov(x) plot_rank_mobility(x, geometry = NULL)

what: What Seems Real After The Audit
title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: R pieces workflow: Academic R implementations exist, especially estdaR spdyn, maintained, CRAN-grade, tidy/sf-native user products. sfdep spdep cover spatial weights, lags, local spatial statistics, transition dynamics distributions time. Generic Markov chains exist, applied spatial Markov workflows tied long sf panels maps. Sequence analysis exists, geospatial distribution dynamics named package concept. Visualization packages exist, purpose-built maps/heatmaps/trajectory plots methods.

title: Pre-Scoping Document: griddy, Geospatial Distribution Dynamics for R
text: Default proposal: pooled quantiles across time periods. Reason: pooled cutpoints make class meanings comparable time. Year-specific quantiles useful rank-relative mobility can hide absolute change. Need clear docs classification choice materially changes conclusions.
